laws.title: Public Law 15-101 | Guidance to Guidances Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is readable and general-and-permanent. It governs agency guidance publication, FOIA handling, and related definitions keyed to 5 U.S.C. 551 and 553, but it does not present a clean positive-law rewrite target.

current_implementation.detail: No law-specific insertion or note id was found in usc/usc05.xml, and no PL-015-101 delta was identifiable in the origin/main comparison.

source_evidence.status_basis: The extracted enactment text contains a short title, definitions, publication duties, FOIA carve-out, severability, and effective date.

laws.recommended_action: If this enactment is to be carried into the Code, add a concise title 5 statutory note rather than a pasted amendment block.

laws.title: Public Law 15-102 | New Ambassadorial Organizational and Reporting Act
laws.status_basis: The source creates regional ambassadorships and weekly reporting duties and expressly repeals the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act and PL 14-90 section 2(a)(i). It remains an active enactment, but it reads as note material rather than a direct Code rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-102 statutory-note or repeal marker was found in usc/usc22.xml, and the branch-vs-origin comparison did not surface a law-specific insertion.

source_evidence.status_basis: The PDF text includes a short title, severability, effective date, express repeal language, regional ambassadorship creation, and weekly reporting duties.

laws.recommended_action: Add a concise title 22 statutory note and place repeal markers at the affected earlier-law homes.

laws.title: Public Law 15-104 | The Military Justice Reform Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is a broad military-justice reorganization with CJAG, JAG, DJAG, courts, ethics, and paralegal provisions. It does not supply a clean positive-law insertion target and is better preserved as historical or note material.

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-104 insertion was found in usc/usc10.xml, and no matching branch-only change was identified against origin/main.

source_evidence.status_basis: The extracted text establishes military-justice offices and duties but does not map cleanly to a stable title 10 amendment.

laws.recommended_action: Retain the enactment as non-code history rather than inserting the bill text into permanent title 10 sections.

laws.title: Public Law 15-105 | Municipal Prosecution Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source shifts District of Columbia prosecution and recall-petition duties and expressly repeals Part D of PL 12-88, Title 6 section 37 of PL 12-88, and later provisions tied to PL 15-97. It is operative, but the work is note-and-repeal markup rather than a full code rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-105 note or repeal marker was located in usc/usc28.xml, and the current branch did not show a law-specific insertion relative to origin/main.

source_evidence.status_basis: The enactment text contains a repealment section, a District of Columbia prosecution section, a recall-process section, and a later repeal/substitution clause.

laws.recommended_action: Place a concise statutory note at the title 28 anchor and add repeal markers at the earlier-law homes that the text expressly repeals.

laws.title: Public Law 15-106 | The Congress Strikes Back Repealment
laws.status_basis: The source is a repealer statute whose only substantive effect is to repeal The Congress Strikes Back Act of 2022. It remains current as a repealing enactment, but its Code treatment is historical-note material tied to the repealed act.
targets: The Congress Strikes Back Act of 2022

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-106 note was found in usc/usc02.xml, and the branch-vs-origin comparison did not identify a law-specific insertion.

source_evidence.status_basis: The text contains a short title, severability, effective date, and an express repeal of The Congress Strikes Back Act.

laws.recommended_action: Record the repeal at the target law's home and keep this enactment only as repealing history.

laws.title: Public Law 15-107 | Guidance to Guidances Act
laws.status_basis: No readable authenticated enactment text was recovered from the supplied source set, so no provision-level codification conclusion can be asserted safely.

current_implementation.detail: The source is unrecoverable, so no verified XML placement can be audited.

source_evidence.status_basis: The repaired source metadata marks the law unrecoverable and provides no usable source files.

laws.recommended_action: Retain only a source-limited historical note until the actual enactment text is recovered.

laws.title: Public Law 15-108 | The Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is an internal congressional labeling statute. It prescribes bill, resolution, amendment, committee-report, and treaty labels, but it does not enact positive-law Code text.

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-108 insertion or note id was found in usc/usc02.xml, and no law-specific branch delta appeared in the origin/main comparison.

source_evidence.status_basis: The extracted HTML text is a labeling and organization statute with no express Code amendment.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the law as non-code history rather than converting it into permanent Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 15-109 | Ending Tigray for the Last Time
laws.status_basis: The resolution is current, but its sole operative effect is to repeal House Joint Resolution 6 in full. That makes it a historical repealer, not positive-law Code text.

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-109 note or repeal marker was found in the branch XML, and no law-specific insertion was visible in the origin/main comparison.

source_evidence.status_basis: The text states that the formal notice of war in House Joint Resolution 6 is repealed in its entirety.

laws.recommended_action: Preserve the resolution only as repealing history and attach the repeal to the repealed instrument's home.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It defines presidential-transition obstruction, sets a criminal prohibition, and supplies a short jail/fine framework. It belongs as note material at title 18 rather than as stray generic text.

current_implementation.detail: No PL-015-111 insertion or note id was found in usc/usc18.xml, and no law-specific branch delta was visible relative to origin/main.

source_evidence.status_basis: The enactment text contains a short title, definitions, an obstruction prohibition, penalties, and an immediate-effective-date clause.

laws.recommended_action: Preserve the enactment as a concise title 18 statutory note, not as a free-floating generic anchor.
